tools.name: find_images
tools.description: Search for images by class name, object type, or natural language description. Use this for initial queries. Returns a visual grid.

query.description: Natural language search query or class name (e.g., 'dogs', 'person riding horse', 'musical instruments')

limit.description: Maximum number of images to return per page (1-50)

relationship.description: Optional relationship filter to narrow results (e.g., 'ride', 'wears', 'holds'). Excludes 'is' by default.

min_confidence.description: Minimum confidence threshold for label matches (0.0-1.0). Omit for all confidence levels.

outputSchema.description: Three-layer response: structuredContent for model-readable summary, content for conversation text, _meta for widget-exclusive data

structuredContent.description: Model-readable structured summary of search results. Rendered as the widget's primary data source.

query.description: Echo of the original search query for display and re-query

total_results.description: Total number of matching images across all pages

facets.description: Available narrowing dimensions derived from result set

categories.description: Subcategories from class_hierarchy children of the matched class. Each entry has name and count.

relationships.description: Relationship types present in results, excluding 'is' by default. Each entry has label and count.

confidence_ranges.description: Distribution of confidence scores across results for filter suggestions

summary.description: Human-readable one-line summary (e.g., '523 dog images found across 12 breeds')

content.description: Conversation text describing results with narrowing suggestions. Shown in the chat as the model's response.

text.description: Natural language description of results including counts, top categories, and suggested refinements

_meta.description: Widget-exclusive data not shown in conversation text. Passed to the widget iframe for rendering.

images.description: Image result set for the current page

labels.description: Human-readable label names for this image

confidence.description: Highest confidence score among matching labels

hierarchy_context.description: Position in the class hierarchy for the queried class

path.description: Root path from class_hierarchy (e.g., 'Entity > Animal > Carnivore > Dog')

query_metadata.description: Execution metadata for debugging and performance monitoring

execution_time_ms.description: Server-side query execution time in milliseconds

tools.name: narrow_results
tools.description: Refine the current search results by adding filters. Called by the widget directly -- do not call this from conversation.

previous_query.description: The original search query being refined

filter.description: Filter expression. Supports: 'category:Poodle' (class filter), 'relationship:ride' (relationship type), 'confidence:>0.8' (threshold), or free text for additional search terms.

limit.description: Maximum number of images per page (1-50)

outputSchema.description: Same three-layer structure as find_images. The widget re-renders in place with filtered results.

structuredContent.description: Updated structured summary reflecting applied filters

applied_filters.description: List of currently active filters for display as removable chips

tools.name: get_image_details
tools.description: Get full details for a single image including all annotations, bounding boxes, relationships, and metadata.

outputSchema.description: Three-layer response with complete image annotations and navigation actions

structuredContent.description: Core image data visible to the model for conversation context

metadata.description: Image metadata from the images table

content.description: Natural language description of the image contents synthesized from annotations

text.description: Describes what is in the image: objects, relationships, and scene composition

_meta.description: Complete annotation data for the widget to render overlays and panels

labels.description: All image-level labels with source and confidence

boxes.description: Bounding box annotations for rendering overlays on the image

relationships.description: Visual relationships between objects in the image

masks.description: Segmentation masks available for this image

mask_path.description: S3 path to segmentation mask PNG

navigate_actions.description: Pre-computed navigation suggestions for 'explore from this image' actions

more_like_this.description: Query to find visually similar images (primary label as search term)

same_objects.description: Queries to find images with the same objects

same_relationships.description: Queries to find images with the same relationship patterns

same_category.description: Query to browse the parent category of the primary object

tools.name: explore_category
tools.description: Browse the class hierarchy tree starting from a category. Shows subcategories, sample images, and available relationships.

class_name.description: Human-readable class name to explore (e.g., 'Animal', 'Dog', 'Vehicle'). Uses display_name, not MID.

depth.description: How many hierarchy levels below the class to include (1-5)

include_samples.description: Whether to include sample thumbnail images for each subcategory

outputSchema.description: Three-layer response with hierarchy tree data and sample images

structuredContent.description: Hierarchy summary visible to the model for guiding exploration

root_path.description: Full path from Entity root (e.g., 'Entity > Animal > Carnivore > Dog')

depth.description: Number of levels shown below the class

content.description: Natural language description of the category with subcategories and relationship context

text.description: Description like 'Dog is under Entity > Animal > Carnivore with 15 subcategories including Poodle, German shepherd...'

_meta.description: Full hierarchy tree and sample images for the widget to render

hierarchy_tree.description: Recursive tree structure for rendering as collapsible tree

children.description: Recursive children, each with the same structure

sample_count.description: Number of images with this label in the dataset

relationship_summary.description: Relationships involving this class or its descendants, excluding 'is'

sample_images.description: 3-5 sample thumbnails per subcategory (when include_samples is true)

prompts.name: start_code_mode
prompts.description: Switch to code mode for advanced SQL queries against the Open Images database. Loads the schema reference and enables SQL generation for queries that go beyond the standard tools.

resource_context.description: Complete schema reference with table/view definitions, common values, query patterns, and known pitfalls

rules: Replace __DATABASE__ with open_images in all generated queries
Use Athena/Trino SQL dialect (e.g., LIMIT not TOP, double quotes for identifiers)
Prefer views (labeled_images, labeled_boxes, labeled_masks, labeled_relationships, class_hierarchy, hierarchy_relationships) over base tables for human-readable names
Use hierarchy_relationships with ancestor_name for parent-class queries (e.g., ancestor_name_1 = 'Person' matches Man, Woman, Boy, Girl)
Never query relationships by parent class using display_name_1 directly -- use ancestor_name_1 from hierarchy_relationships
Filter out 'is' relationship type by default unless the user specifically asks about attributes/states
Use display_name columns (not label_name/MID) in WHERE clauses and output
The 'clicks' column in masks is VARCHAR (semicolon-delimited), not JSON -- use split(clicks, ';') and cardinality()
pitfalls: MIDs vs display_name: label_name contains MIDs like /m/04yx4, not human-readable names. Always JOIN class_descriptions or use views.
'is' dominance: 81.8% of relationships are 'is' type (attributes). Filter with WHERE relationship_label != 'is' for action/spatial relationships.
Leaf-only relationships: The relationships table only contains leaf classes (Man, Woman, Dog). To query by parent class (Person, Animal), use hierarchy_relationships view with ancestor_name columns.
INNER JOIN drops: Views using INNER JOIN with class_descriptions drop ~3.3% of rows. All dropped rows are 'is' type relationships.
edge_type: Some classes appear under multiple parents with different edge_types ('subcategory' vs 'part'). Filter on edge_type if needed.
